title: Rachida Dati
wikitext:

[[Michel Barnier]]
[[François Bayrou]]
[[Sébastien Lecornu]] | predecessor = [[Rima Abdul Malak]] | successor = [[Catherine Pégard]] | office3 = [[Mayor (France)|Mayor]] of the [[7th arrondissement of Paris]] | term_start3 = 29 March 2008 | term_end3 = | predecessor3 = Michel Dumont | office1 = | term_start1 = | term_end1 = | constituency1 = | office2 = [[Minister of Justice (France)|Minister of Justice]] | term_start2 = 18 May 2007 | term_end2 = 23 June 2009 | primeminister2 = [[François Fillon]] | predecessor2 = [[Pascal Clément]] | successor2 = [[Michèle Alliot-Marie]] | office4 = [[Council of Paris|Councillor of Paris]] | term_start4 = 21 March 2008 | constituency4 = [[7th arrondissement of Paris|7th arrondissement]] | birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1965|11|27|df=y}} | birth_place = [[Saint-Rémy, Saône-et-Loire|Saint-Rémy]], [[France]] | death_date = | death_place = | party = [[The Republicans (France)|LR]] (2015–2024; since 2025) | otherparty = [[Union for a Popular Movement|UMP]] (2006–2015)
[[Independent politician|Independent]] (2024–2025) | education = | children = 1 | occupation = [[Lawyer]] • [[Magistrate]] • [[Politician]] | spouse = {{marriage|Unknown|1992|1995|end=annulled}} | citizenship = France • Morocco | alma_mater = [[University of Burgundy]] ([[Master of Economics|MAEs]])
[[Paris-Panthéon-Assas University|Panthéon-Assas University]] ([[Bachelor of Laws|LLB]]) | office5 = [[Member of the European Parliament]] | constituency5 = [[Île-de-France (European Parliament constituency)|Île-de-France]] | term_start5 = 14 July 2009 | term_end5 = 1 July 2019 }} '''Rachida Dati''' ({{IPA|fr|ʁaʃida dati||LL-Q150 (fra)-Jérémy-Günther-Heinz Jähnick-Rachida Dati.wav}}; born 27 November 1965) is a French politician, lawyer and magistrate who served as [[Minister of Culture (France)|Minister of Culture]] from 2024 to 2026 in the [[Attal government|government]] of [[Gabriel Attal]], [[Barnier government|government]] of [[Michel Barnier]], [[Bayrou government|government]] of [[François Bayrou]] and the [[First Lecornu government|first]] and [[Second Lecornu government|second]] governments of [[Sébastien Lecornu]]. She previously was [[Minister of Justice (France)|Minister of Justice]] from 2007 to 2009 under Prime Minister [[François Fillon]]. A member of [[The Republicans (France)|The Republicans]] (LR), Dati was a [[Member of the European Parliament]] (MEP) from 2009 to 2019, representing [[Île-de-France (European Parliament constituency)|Île-de-France]]. She was a spokesperson for [[Nicolas Sarkozy]] during his [[2007 French presidential election|2007]] presidential campaign; following his victory, he appointed her to the [[Government of France|Government]]. Dati was elected to the mayorship of the [[7th arrondissement of Paris]] in 2008, when she also entered the [[Council of Paris]]. In the [[2020 Paris municipal election]], she unsuccessfully ran for [[Mayor of Paris]] against incumbent [[Anne Hidalgo]]. Following the election, she was installed as opposition leader in the Council of Paris. She again ran in [[2026 Paris municipal election|2026]], placing second to [[Socialist Party (France)|socialist]] [[Emmanuel Grégoire]]. ==Early life and education== Rachida Dati ({{langx|ar|رشيدة داتي}}) was born on 27 November 1965 in [[Saint-Rémy, Saône-et-Loire|Saint-Rémy]], [[Burgundy]], to a [[Morocco|Moroccan]] father, a [[bricklayer]] named M'Barek Dati (1934–2017), and an [[Algeria]]n mother, named Fatima-Zohra (died in 2001). Her parents [[Immigration to France|immigrated to France]] in 1963.{{Cite web |date=2024-01-12 |title=Qui est Malika Dati, la sœur aînée de Rachida Dati ? |url=https://www.ohmymag.com/people/actu-people/rachida-dati-ministre-de-la-culture-qui-est-sa-soeur-malika-dati-aussi-dans-la-politique_art163033.html |access-date=2024-01-17 |website=Ohmymag |language=fr}} She was the second child of eleven in an impoverished family (eight girls and three boys). She spent her childhood in [[Chalon-sur-Saône]].{{in lang|fr}} [https://france3-regions.francetvinfo.fr/bourgogne-franche-comte/saone-et-loire/chalon-sur-saone/municipales-2020-chalonnaise-rachida-dati-investie-paris-republicains-1746269.html "Municipales 2020 : la Chalonnaise Rachida Dati investie à Paris par Les Républicains"], france3-regions.francetvinfo.fr, 7 November 2019.{{Cite web |last=Purepeople |title=Deuxième d'une fratrie de onze enfants, Rachida Dati a toujours été très proche de sa famille. Exclusif - Rachida Dati avec sa famille et ses proches lors d'un brunch familial à la brasserie Le Comptoir Principal dans le 15ème arrondissement à Paris le 26 janvier 2020. Ses cinq soeurs Fatiha, Nadia, Malika, Noura, Najat et son frère étaient présents. En bas à gauche de l'image, sa fille Zohra de dos. © Alain Guizard / Bestimage - Photo |url=https://www.purepeople.com/media/deuxieme-d-une-fratrie-de-onze-enfants_m7359517 |access-date=2024-01-21 |website=www.purepeople.com|language=fr}}{{Cite web |last=Média |first=Prisma |date=2009-10-14 |title=Rachida Dati, retour en grâce au Grévin - Gala |url=https://www.gala.fr/l_actu/news_de_stars/rachida_dati_retour_en_grace_au_grevin_189429 |access-date=2024-01-21 |website=Gala.fr |language=fr}} Even though Dati was raised in a devout Islamic environment, she attended [[Catholic school|Roman Catholic school]]s; Dati's own personal religious beliefs have been described as "unclear".{{Cite web|date=2020-05-29|title=Sarkozy minister Rachida Dati targets Paris Mayor job|first1=Angela|last1=Diffley|url=https://www.rfi.fr/en/france/20200529-sarkozy-s-rachida-dati-muslim-mayor-paris|access-date=2022-02-20|website=RFI|language=en}} When asked about her North African origins, she stated she saw herself first and foremost as a "daughter of France".{{in lang|fr}} [https://www.lepoint.fr/politique/rachida-dati-mechamment-douee-08-12-2022-2500896_20.php "Rachida Dati, méchamment douée"], ''[[Le Point]]'', 8 December 2022. Dati studied at the [[University of Burgundy]] in [[Dijon]], where she received a [[master's degree]] in [[economics]], as well as at [[Paris-Panthéon-Assas University|Panthéon-Assas University]] in [[Paris]], where she later received a [[Validation des Acquis de l'Expérience|law degree]].{{cite book|last=Remy|first=Jacqueline|title=Du Rimmel et des larmes|year=2009|publisher=Seuil|page=52|language=French}} ==Early career== At the age of sixteen, Dati started working as a [[maid]] and as a [[paramedic]]al assistant. She then worked for three years as an [[accountant]] at [[Elf Aquitaine]] while at university. Rachida Dati tried to study [[medicine]], but failed twice in her first year. In October 1985, she completed a [[Diplôme d'études universitaires générales|DEUG]] in [[economics]] at the [[University of Burgundy|University of Dijon]].{{Cite web |date=2008-09-06 |title=Just who is Rachida Dati? |url=https://www.arabnews.com/node/315814 |access-date=2024-01-13 |website=Arab News |language=en}} After meeting [[Jean-Luc Lagardère]] in 1990, Dati entered the audit management team of [[Matra#Hautes Technologies|Matra Nortel communication]]. She later spent a year in [[London]] at the [[European Bank for Reconstruction and Development]], in the records management and archiving department. In 1994, she was an auditing supervisor and secretary-general of the bureau of urban development studies at [[Suez (company, 1997–2008)|Suez]] (then Lyonnaise des Eaux). From 1995 to 1997, she worked as a technical advisor at the legal management division of the [[Minister of National Education (France)|Ministry of Education]].{{Cite web |last=Auffray |first=Alain |title=Dati, l'ascension courtisane |url=https://www.liberation.fr/france/2009/02/23/dati-l-ascension-courtisane_311813/ |access-date=2024-03-05 |website=Libération |language=fr}} In 1997, following the advice of [[Simone Veil]] and [[Albin Chalandon]], Dati was admitted to and joined the [[French National School for the Judiciary|École nationale de la magistrature]] (French National School for the Judiciary), a public educational institution which offers courses necessary to become a magistrate. Upon leaving in 1999, she became a legal auditor at the [[Bobigny]] ''tribunal de grande instance'' (high court). She went on to become a judge for collective proceduresEquivalent to [[United States bankruptcy court|bankruptcy courts]] in the US. at the ''{{lang|fr|tribunal de grande instance}}'' in [[Péronne, Somme|Péronne]] and eventually an assistant in the financial section to the prosecutor of the [[Évry, Essonne|Évry]] ''{{lang|fr|tribunal de grande instance}}''. ==Career in politics== In 2002, Dati became [[Nicolas Sarkozy]]'s advisor, working for him on an anti-delinquency project. In 2006, she joined the [[Union for a Popular Movement]] (UMP) party. On 14 January 2007, she was named spokesperson for Sarkozy on the day he was chosen as UMP candidate for the presidential elections of April 2007. ===Minister of Justice, 2007–2009=== After Sarkozy's victory on 6 May 2007, she was appointed [[Minister of Justice (France)|Minister of Justice]], making her the first political figure born to [[North Africa]]n immigrant parents to occupy a sovereign ministry in a [[Government of France|French government]]. Her rationalization of the court system was publicly opposed by judicial professionals.Kerdreux, Gilles "[http://www.lemonde.fr/cgi-bin/ACHATS/1012208.html?offre=ARCHIVES&type_item=ART_ARCH_30J&objet_id=1012208 Mme Dati affronte un mécontentement croissant sur la carte judiciaire]", LeMonde.fr 11 November 2007 Later on, it was recognised by the French Court of Auditors as one of the most ambitious reforms of the judicial institution.{{Cite web|url=https://www.lesechos.fr/11/02/2015/lesechos.fr/0204151862516_la-carte-judiciaire-erigee-en-modele-de-reforme-par-la-cour-des-comptes.htm|title=La carte judiciaire érigée en modèle de réforme par la Cour des comptes - Les Echos|website=www.lesechos.fr|date=11 February 2015|language=fr|access-date=7 February 2018}}{{Dead link|date=July 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} When the Sarkozys' marriage began to break up, Dati frequently went on official presidential trips to accompany Nicolas Sarkozy.Angelique Chrisafis (20 November 2008), [https://www.theguardian.com/world/2008/nov/20/rachida-dati-france-sarkozy-pregnant The rise and fall of Rachida Dati] ''[[The Guardian]]''. On 23 January 2009, Sarkozy announced that Dati would take the second position on the UMP candidate list for the [[Île-de-France]] constituency in the [[2009 European Parliament election in France|European Parliament election]] in June 2009, to which she was elected.[http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/world/europe/7847997.stm France's Dati to quit government], ''BBC News'' Retrieved on 23 January 2009 She left her post as minister after being elected as a [[Member of the European Parliament]]. Soon after she left the government, in the summer of 2009, Dati switched to law, becoming a junior magistrate and assistant prosecutor.David Keohane (5 June 2019), [https://www.ft.com/content/73bc0c42-878c-11e9-a028-86cea8523dc2 Ex-minister probed over Renault-Nissan payments] ''[[Financial Times]]''. She also founded a consulting company called "La Bourdonnais consultant,"François Labrouillère, [http://www.parismatch.com/Actu-Match/Politique/Actu/Info-Paris-Match-Rachida-Dati-cree-sa-societe-de-consultants-115226/] "Rachida Dati crée sa société de consultants", Paris Match, 23 juillet 2009 which she had to dissolve at the beginning of 2010 to be able to resume the profession of lawyer, which she had to do by special dispensation (like other former magistrates).{{Cite web|url=https://parislightsup.com/2019/09/15/whats-happening-in-the-paris-mayoral-elections/|title=The Paris Mayoral Election Explained|date=15 September 2019}} She sits on the editorial board of the French version of the ''[[Huffington Post]]'', where she writes a weekly column about women's issues.Renaud Revel, 'Rachida Dati: "Arianna Huffington est venue me voir à la mairie"', in ''[[L'Express]]'', 24 January 2012 [http://www.lexpress.fr/actualite/media-people/media/rachida-dati-anne-sinclair-est-une-immernse-professionnelle_1074784.html] ===Member of the European Parliament, 2009–2019=== A member of the [[European People's Party group]] in the European Parliament,{{Cite web|url=https://www.rachida-dati.eu/parlement-europeen|title=Parlement Européen {{!}} Rachida Dati|website=www.rachida-dati.eu|language=fr|access-date=7 February 2018}} Dati served on the [[Committee on Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs]] and the parliament's delegations for relations with the Mashreq countries, to the [[Parliamentary Assembly of the Mediterranean|Parliamentary Assembly]] of the [[Union for the Mediterranean]], and for relations with the [[Arab Peninsula]]. In parliament, Dati was the Parliament's [[rapporteur]] on several texts dealing with countering terrorism and the prevention of radicalisation and recruitment of European citizens by terrorist organisations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.europarl.europa.eu/meps/fr/72775/RACHIDA_DATI_home.html|title=Rachida DATI {{!}} Accueil {{!}} Députés {{!}} Parlement européen|website=www.europarl.europa.eu|language=fr|access-date=7 February 2018}} Following the [[Charlie Hebdo shooting]] in 2015, she drafted a report into how to prevent the radicalisation of young Europeans.Alastair Macdonald (25 November 2015), [https://www.reuters.com/article/france-shooting-eu-parliament/eu-lawmakers-call-for-blacklist-of-european-jihadists-idUSL8N13K4KI20151125 EU lawmakers call for "blacklist" of European jihadists] ''[[Reuters]]''. The [https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:52015IP0410 final report] was adopted on the 25th of November 2015 with broad support from the parliament after having passed ''[https://www.eumonitor.nl/9353000/1/j9vvik7m1c3gyxp/vjybdyrvhgsh?ctx=vkgmeols6czl&s0e=vhdubxdwqrzw The Civil Liberties Committee approved the non-binding resolution by 41 votes to 7, with 6 abstentions]''. Her parliamentary work also included dealing with the prison systems and conditions in the European Union, and finding solutions to face the migration crisis with an EU common list of safe countries of origin.{{Cite web|url=http://www.europarl.europa.eu/meps/fr/72775/RACHIDA_DATI_activities.html|title=Rachida DATI {{!}} Activités parlementaires {{!}} Députés {{!}} Parlement européen|website=www.europarl.europa.eu|language=fr|access-date=7 February 2018}} In the UMP's [[2012 Union for a Popular Movement leadership election|2012 leadership election]], Dati endorsed [[Jean-François Copé]].[https://www.nouvelobs.com/politique/bataille-ump/20121017.OBS5966/cope-fillon-et-l-ump-qui-soutient-qui.html Copé, Fillon et l'UMP : qui soutient qui ?] ''[[L'Obs]]'', 17 October 2012. In the Republicans’ [[2017 The Republicans (France) leadership election|2017 leadership election]], Dati endorsed [[Laurent Wauquiez]].Ludovic Vigogne (11 October 2017), [https://www.lopinion.fr/edition/politique/liste-136-parrains-laurent-wauquiez-135723 La liste des 136 parrains de Laurent Wauquiez] ''[[L'Opinion (French newspaper)|L'Opinion]]''. ===Career in local politics=== On the local level, Dati has been serving as Mayor of the 7th district of Paris and a member of the [[Council of Paris]].{{Cite web|url=https://parislightsup.com/2019/09/15/whats-happening-in-the-paris-mayoral-elections/|title=The Paris Mayoral Election Explained|date=15 September 2019}} On 9 February 2013, Dati announced she was a candidate for [[mayor of Paris]] in the 2014 local elections but she later withdrew because "the press has already chosen [[Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet]]". In early 2019, Dati announced her plan to run again for the [[2020 French municipal elections|Paris municipal election]] in 2020.Emmanuel Jarry (4 June 2019), [https://www.reuters.com/article/us-renault-nissan/former-french-justice-minister-dati-probed-over-fees-paid-by-renault-nissan-idUSKCN1T52W7 Former French Justice minister Dati probed over fees paid by Renault-Nissan] ''[[Reuters]]''. Since 2020, she has been chairing her party's group in the Council of Paris.Marie-Anne Gairaud and Christine Henry (23 July 2020), [https://www.leparisien.fr/paris-75/conseil-de-paris-qui-sont-les-six-nouveaux-presidents-de-groupe-23-07-2020-8357496.php Conseil de Paris : qui sont les six nouveaux présidents de groupe] ''[[Le Parisien]]''. Dati was a candidate for the 2026 Paris mayoral election,https://rachidadati2026.com/ running on the programme "Change Paris" focused on security, cleanliness, and budgetary responsibility. She was endorsed by the conservative Republican party. ===Minister of Culture, 2024–2026=== On 11 January 2024, Dati made a surprise comeback to national politics, being nominated as [[Ministry of Culture (France)|Minister of Culture]] in the [[Attal government|government]] of [[Gabriel Attal]].{{cite news|url=https://www.artnews.com/art-news/news/rachida-dati-right-wing-politician-named-france-new-culture-minister-1234692847/|author=Melanie Goodfellow|date=January 12, 2024|title=Controversial Right-wing Politician Named France's New Culture Minister|work=ART News}}{{cite news|url=https://deadline.com/2024/01/controversial-politician-rachida-dati-france-new-culture-minister-1235717301/|author=Melanie Goodfellow|date=January 11, 2024|title=Controversial Politician Rachida Dati Appointed France's New Culture Minister|work=Deadline}} As a consequence, [[The Republicans (France)|Les Républicains]] President [[Éric Ciotti]] announced her exclusion from the party.{{Cite web |url=https://www.ladepeche.fr/2024/01/11/rachida-dati-ministre-de-la-culture-nous-tirons-les-consequences-de-son-choix-avec-regret-eric-ciotti-annonce-son-exclusion-des-lr-11690457.php |title=Rachida Dati ministre de la Culture : "Nous tirons les conséquences de son choix avec regret", Eric Ciotti annonce son exclusion des LR |date=2024-01-11 |website=La Dépêche |language=fr}}{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/france-culture-minister-rachida-dati-first-address-movies-save-lives-1235877672/|title=France's New Culture Minister Rachida Dati Says Movies 'Save Lives' in First Address to Entertainment Industry Players|date=19 January 2024 |publisher=Variety}} She retained her position on 21 September 2024 in the [[Barnier government|government]] of [[Michel Barnier]], and then on 23 December in the [[Bayrou government|government]] of [[François Bayrou]].{{Cite web |url=https://www.lemonde.fr/societe/article/2024/09/22/rachida-dati-une-ministre-confirmee-dans-le-gouvernement-barnier-malgre-l-epee-de-damocles-de-la-justice_6328440_3224.html |title= Rachida Dati, une ministre confirmée dans le gouvernement Barnier malgré l'épée de Damoclès de la justice |date=2024-09-22 |website=[[Le Monde]] |language=fr}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.francetvinfo.fr/culture/gouvernement-bayrou-rachida-dati-reconduite-a-la-culture_6974240.html |title= Gouvernement Bayrou : Rachida Dati reconduite à la Culture |date=2024-12-24 |website=[[France Info]] |language=fr}} She again retained her position in the [[First Lecornu government|first]] and [[Second Lecornu government|second]] governments of [[Sébastien Lecornu]].{{Cite web |title=La composition du gouvernement de Sébastien Lecornu |url=https://www.info.gouv.fr/actualite/la-composition-du-gouvernement-de-sebastien-lecornu |access-date=2025-10-14 |website=info.gouv.fr |language=fr}}{{Cite web |title=Composition du gouvernement de Sébastien Lecornu |url=https://www.info.gouv.fr/actualite/composition-du-gouvernement-du-sebastien-lecornu |access-date=2025-10-14 |website=info.gouv.fr |language=fr}} On 19 October 2025, the [[2025 Louvre robbery]] happened, with Dati testifying in the investigation. She resigned on 25 February 2026 in order to run in the [[2026 Paris municipal election]], succeeded by [[Catherine Pégard]],{{Cite web |last=Keslassy |first=Elsa |date=2026-02-26 |title=Former Château de Versailles President Catherine Pégard Appointed France’s Culture Minister, Succeeding Rachida Dati |url=https://variety.com/2026/film/global/catherine-pegard-france-culture-minister-rachida-dati-1236673764/ |access-date=2026-02-26 |website=Variety |language=en-US}} losing the 22 March mayoral race to the Socialist candidate [[Emmanuel Grégoire]].{{cite news |last=Chrisafis |first=Angelique |location=Paris|date=23 March 2026 |title=Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire elected Paris mayor, as National Rally fails to take key cities |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/22/paris-mayoral-race-socialist-emmanuel-gregoire-projected-to-win |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=23 March 2026}} ==Controversy== In early 2009, Dati received an anonymous death threat accompanied by a 9 mm bullet.{{Cite news |last=Davies |first=Lizzy |date=2009-03-03 |title=Five French rightwing politicians receive death threats |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2009/mar/03/france-sarkozy-death-threats |access-date=2024-01-17 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}} Soon after Dati left the government in 2009 to stand for the European Parliament, she was hired by the [[Renault–Nissan–Mitsubishi Alliance]] as a legal advisor.Laurence Frost (1 February 2019), [https://www.reuters.com/article/us-nissan-ghosn-rnbv/renault-nissan-payments-to-political-advisers-draw-scrutiny-idUSKCN1PQ596 Renault-Nissan payments to political advisers draw scrutiny]. ''[[Reuters]]''. In 2019, France's financial prosecutor launched an investigation into consulting fees she received from the alliance.Emmanuel Jarry (4 June 2019), [https://www.reuters.com/article/us-renault-nissan/former-french-justice-minister-dati-probed-over-fees-paid-by-renault-nissan-idUSKCN1T52W7 Former French Justice minister Dati probed over fees paid by Renault-Nissan]. ''[[Reuters]]''. In December 2013, French media reported that Dati had received payments from French energy utility [[Engie|GDF-Suez]]. In early 2014, the [[President of the European Parliament]] [[Martin Schulz]] asked parliamentary services to look into conflict-of-interest concerns, but the inquiry was interrupted by the 2014 election campaign. At the same time, the independent French administrative authority [[Haute Autorité pour la transparence de la vie publique|HATVP]], France's anti-corruption watchdog, also opened a file on the case.Quentin Ariès and James Panichi (9 September 2015), [https://www.politico.eu/article/ex-mep-speaks-out-on-decision-not-to-pursue-dati-allegations/ Ex-MEP speaks out on decision not to pursue Dati allegations]. ''[[Politico Europe]]''. In August 2021, Dati was charged by France's financial crime unit with passive corruption and benefiting from abuse of power.France24, [https://www.france24.com/en/france/20210727-former-french-minister-rachida-dati-charged-with-corruption-in-ghosn-case Former French Minister Rachida Dati charged with corruption in Ghosn case]. ''[[France 24]]'' On 27 September 2021, [[Arte]] reported how [[caviar diplomacy]] led to the rejection of a report on [[Azerbaijan]]i political prisoners by the European Parliament in 2013. The claim was made that the rejection was due to bribery of EU parliamentarians; Dati stood out as one of the leading voices to reject the report about the state of democracy in Azerbaijan. Her Italian colleague [[Luca Volontè]] was sentenced for accepting bribes. Volontè received €2.4 million as bribes from a 30 million-euro bribe fund of the Azerbaijani fund to thwart EU guidelines by bribing its institutions.{{Cite web|title=Die Kaviar-Connection - Die Bestechungsmaschine - Dokumentation, Arte, 28. Sep., 21:10 Uhr - TV Programm {{!}} tvdirekt.de|url=https://www.tvdirekt.de/tv-programm/sendungsdetails/166613516/die-kaviar-connection-die-bestechungsmaschine.html|access-date=2021-09-29|website=www.tvdirekt.de|archive-date=29 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210929123049/https://www.tvdirekt.de/tv-programm/sendungsdetails/166613516/die-kaviar-connection-die-bestechungsmaschine.html|url-status=dead}}{{Cite web|title=La caviar connection (1/2) - Le pouvoir de l'argent - Regarder le documentaire complet|url=https://www.arte.tv/fr/videos/096318-000-A/la-caviar-connection-1-2/|access-date=2021-09-29|website=ARTE|language=fr}} === 2025 Musée du Louvre jewellery theft === Under her direction, on the morning of 19 October 2025 the Musée du Louvre announced the museum would be closed for the day "for exceptional reasons". According to the French Ministry of Culture and media reports, the theft occurred at the gallery known as the Galerie d’Apollon, where jewellery from the French Crown-Jewels collection — including nine items said to belong to Napoleon and the Empress, such as a necklace, tiara and brooch — were on display. French authorities launched an investigation. The Culture Minister, Rachida Dati, stated she was on-site with museum staff and police. At the time of the report, the full list of stolen items, precise value and suspect details were not publicly disclosed. The stolen objects are part of the historic collection of the French Crown Jewels and imperial jewellery, held at one of the foremost museums in the world. The incident raises significant concerns regarding the security of national patrimony and cultural heritage. For background, the French Crown Jewels are held today in the Louvre among other collections.https://www.ansa.it/sito/notizie/mondo/africa/2025/10/19/rapina-al-louvre-rubati-i-gioielli-di-napoleone.-usato-un-montacarichi-chiuso-il-museo_e12d06cd-8901-4ece-b295-b368a0786b5c.html == Tayeb Benabderrahmane case == {{Main|Tayeb Benabderrahmane v. Qatar}} In 2020, Franco-Algerian lobbyist [[Tayeb Benabderrahmane]] claimed he had been [[Arbitrary arrest and detention|arbitrarily detained]] and tortured in [[Qatar]], in a context linked to his possession of compromising documents concerning [[Nasser Al-Khelaifi|Nasser al-Khelaïfi]], president of [[Paris Saint-Germain FC|Paris Saint-Germain]]. After returning to France, he filed a complaint, leading to the opening of a [[French criminal procedure|judicial investigation]] in Paris for kidnapping, unlawful detention, and extortion in an organized group.{{Cite news |date=2023-06-27 |title=Perquisition à la mairie du 7ᵉ arrondissement de Paris, dirigée par Rachida Dati, et dans plusieurs cabinets d'avocats |url=https://www.lemonde.fr/societe/article/2023/06/27/perquisition-a-la-mairie-du-7-arrondissement-de-paris-dirigee-par-rachida-dati-et-dans-plusieurs-cabinets-d-avocats_6179454_3224.html |access-date=2025-10-03 |language=fr}} On 27 June 2023, the offices of Dati – at the 7th arrondissement town hall and in her law practice – were searched, as were those of lawyers [[Francis Szpiner]] and [[Olivier Pardo]], in connection with the investigation.{{Cite web |date=2023-06-27 |title=Paris: des perquisitions à la mairie du 7e arrondissement et dans deux cabinets d'avocats |url=https://www.bfmtv.com/paris/paris-des-perquisitions-a-la-mairie-du-7e-arrondissement-et-dans-deux-cabinets-d-avocats_AD-202306270775.html |access-date=2025-10-03 |website=BFMTV |language=fr}} The judges examined the alleged role of Dati and Szpiner, then mayor of the 16th arrondissement, in negotiations surrounding the release of Tayeb Benabderrahmane, with the French [[National Financial Prosecutor's Office]] suspecting talks conducted outside the official procedure.{{Cite news |date=2023-06-27 |title=Séquestration supposée d'un lobbyiste au Qatar : le rôle présumé de Rachida Dati et Francis Szpiner en question |url=https://www.lemonde.fr/societe/article/2023/06/27/sequestration-presumee-d-un-lobbyiste-au-qatar-le-role-presume-de-rachida-dati-et-francis-szpiner-en-question_6179486_3224.html |access-date=2025-10-03 |language=fr}} As of 2026, no formal indictment had been announced, but Dati continued to be cited in the affair, which remains under investigation by the French judiciary. At the same time, in the summer of 2025, the [[Working Group on Arbitrary Detention|United Nations Working Group on Arbitrary Detention]] recognized the arbitrary nature of [[Tayeb Benabderrahmane|Benabderrahmane's]] detention in Qatar and called on the Qatari authorities to conduct an investigation and provide reparation for the harm suffered.{{Cite web |last=Gendron |first=Guillaume |title=«Barbouzeries du PSG» : un organe de l'ONU reconnaît la «détention arbitraire» du lobbyiste au cœur de l'affaire qui embarrasse le Qatar et Nasser al-Khelaïfi |url=https://www.liberation.fr/societe/police-justice/barbouzeries-du-psg-un-organe-de-lonu-reconnait-la-detention-arbitraire-du-lobbyiste-au-coeur-de-laffaire-qui-embarrasse-le-qatar-et-nasser-al-khelaifi-20250815_IN6J7OP2XJBCZCLEN6ZUUNF3AU/ |access-date=2025-10-03 |website=Libération |language=fr}} ==Personal life== In November 1992, Rachida Dati married a family friend from [[Algeria]], "with whom she had nothing to share", in her words, to put an end to the "recurring pressures" from her family, what she describes as a "[[forced marriage]]". The following month, she requested the [[annulment]] of this union, which was pronounced in 1995.{{Cite web |last=Média |first=Prisma |date=2021-11-13 |title=Rachida Dati : ce mariage forcé qui l'a tant fait souffrir - Gala |url=https://www.gala.fr/l_actu/news_de_stars/rachida-dati-ce-mariage-force-qui-la-tant-fait-souffrir_480640 |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=Gala.fr |language=fr}} In September 2008, Dati announced that she was pregnant and would be a single mother. She revealed her pregnancy to a group of reporters who questioned her about mounting rumours. "I want to remain careful, because (...) I am still in a [[Complications of pregnancy|risk area]]. I am 42", she was quoted as saying.{{cite news |url=https://www.welt.de/politik/article2392235/Sarkozys-Liebling-Rachida-Dati-ist-schwanger.html |title=Sarkozys Liebling Rachida Dati ist schwanger |newspaper=[[Die Welt]] |date=3 September 2008 }} Her daughter was born in January 2009. As the name of the father was not revealed, many names circulated in [[gossip magazine]]s.{{cite news |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/france/9654427/Rachida-Dati-had-string-of-lovers-in-2008.html |title=Rachida Dati 'had string of lovers in 2008' |first=Devorah |last=Lauter |newspaper=[[The Daily Telegraph]] |date=4 November 2012 |location=London}} Before her daughter was born, she suffered several [[miscarriage]]s.{{Cite web |last=Média |first=Prisma |date=2024-01-15 |title=Rachida Dati mariée de force : "Ils m'ont poussée à le faire…" - Gala |url=https://www.gala.fr/l_actu/news_de_stars/rachida-dati-mariee-de-force-ils-mont-poussee-a-le-faire_480640 |access-date=2024-01-16 |website=Gala.fr |language=fr}}{{Cite web |date=2012-10-03 |title=FRANCE - MAROC. Un juge marocain contre Rachida Dati |url=https://www.courrierinternational.com/breve/2012/10/03/un-juge-marocain-contre-rachida-dati |access-date=2024-01-28 |website=Courrier international |language=fr}} However, in 2012, she started legal action against [[Dominique Desseigne]], the chief executive of [[Groupe Lucien Barrière]], a casino market leader in France, [[Switzerland]] and Europe, in order to establish the paternity of her child.{{Cite web |last=Rédaction |first=La |date=2012-10-04 |title=Rachida Dati : pourquoi le Maroc peut la mettre en prison ? |url=https://www.linternaute.com/actualite/politique/1032308-rachida-dati-pourquoi-le-maroc-peut-la-mettre-en-prison/ |access-date=2024-01-28 |website=www.linternaute.com |language=fr}}{{Cite news|url=http://www.lepoint.fr/societe/assignation-de-desseigne-par-dati-les-preuves-04-10-2012-1513425_23.php|title=EXCLUSIF. Reconnaissance de paternité Desseigne/Dati : la machine judiciaire est lancée|last=Zemouri|first=Jean-Michel Décugis, Aziz|date=4 October 2012|work=Le Point|access-date=7 February 2018|language=fr-FR}}{{Cite news|url=http://www.lemonde.fr/politique/article/2012/11/02/face-au-tourbillon-rachida-dati-un-ex-amant-reste-placide_1784534_823448.html|title=Dominique Desseigne face à Rachida Dati : "Je ne lâcherai rien"|newspaper=Le Monde.fr|date=2 November 2012|language=fr|access-date=7 February 2018}} In December 2012, a French court ordered Desseigne to undergo a [[DNA paternity testing|paternity test]] to see if he fathered Dati's child.{{cite news| url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-20599042 | work=[[BBC News]] | title=Rachida Dati: French court orders tycoon paternity test}} After Desseigne refused to undergo the test, a French court decision of 7 January 2016 ruled that Desseigne was indeed the father.{{cite news|url=http://www.lexpress.fr/actualite/societe/justice/dominique-desseigne-est-bien-le-pere-de-zohra-dati-confirme-la-justice_1752022.html | location=Paris | work=L'Express | title=Dominique Desseigne est bien le père de Zohra Dati, confirme la justice}} In November 2016, she was listed as one of [[100 Women (BBC)|BBC's 100 Women]], having "blazed a trail for Muslim women and minorities in France".{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-38012048|title=BBC 100 Women 2016: Who is on the list?|date=21 November 2016|work=BBC News|access-date=28 July 2019|language=en-GB}} === Religion === Dati was born to [[Islam|Muslim]] parents. However, she attends [[Catholic Church|Roman Catholic]] mass every Sunday.{{Cite book |last=Chavelet |first=Élisabeth |title=Rachida ne meurt jamais |publisher=Editions du Moment |publication-date=7 November 2013 |pages=39 |language=fr |trans-title=Rachida Never Dies}} ==Distinctions== * {{flag|Morocco}}: [[Grand Officer]] of the [[Order of Ouissam Alaouite]] (April 2010)Élisabeth Chavelet, ''Rachida ne meurt jamais'', éditions du Moment, octobre 2013, {{p.|62}}. * {{flagicon|Two Sicilies}} [[House of Bourbon-Two Sicilies|Two Sicilian Royal Family]]: [[Knight]] [[Grand Cross]] of the [[Royal Order of Francis I]]{{cite web|url=http://www.constantinian.org.uk/heir-to-the-french-throne-and-former-french-minister-invested-into-the-order/|title=Heir to the French Throne and former French Minister invested into the Order|website=constantinian.org.uk|date=May 2011}} * {{flag|France}}: [[Commander (order)|Commander]] of the [[Ordre des Arts et des Lettres]] (January 2024)
